---
title: "MP2_APIscraping"
format: html
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(httr)
library(jsonlite)

get_artist_data <- function(artist_name) {
  # 1. SETUP: Identifiers & Rate Limiting
  # MusicBrainz rule: meaningful User-Agent + 1 request per second
  ua <- user_agent("StOlaf_StatsProject/1.0 ( youruser@stolaf.edu )")
  base_url <- "https://musicbrainz.org/ws/2/artist/"
  
  # RULE: We must wait 1 second between calls
  Sys.sleep(1.1)
  
  # 2. SEARCH: Get the MBID for the artist
  # We use the search endpoint first: artist?query=NAME&fmt=json
  search_res <- GET(
    url = base_url,
    query = list(query = artist_name, fmt = "json"),
    ua
  )
  
  # Check if the search worked
  if (status_code(search_res) != 200) return(NULL)
  
  search_data <- fromJSON(content(search_res, as = "text", encoding = "UTF-8"))
  
  # Grab the first artist ID from the results
  artist_id <- search_data$artists$id[1]
  
  if (is.null(artist_id)) return(NULL)
  
  # 3. LOOKUP: Get the actual details using the MBID
  # Endpoint: artist/MBID?fmt=json
  Sys.sleep(1.1) # Respect the rate limit again!
  
  lookup_res <- GET(
    url = paste0(base_url, artist_id),
    query = list(fmt = "json"),
    ua
  )
  
  artist_info <- fromJSON(content(lookup_res, as = "text", encoding = "UTF-8"))
  
  # 4. TIBBLE: Return the data
  tibble(
    artist = artist_name,
    type   = artist_info$type %||% "Unknown",
    gender = artist_info$gender %||% "N/A",
    area   = artist_info$area$name %||% "Unknown",
    start_date = artist_info$`life-span`$begin %||% "N/A"
  )
}

# 5 Artists
target_artists <- c("The Beatles", "Taylor Swift", "Radiohead", "Daft Punk", "Miles Davis")

# Use map_dfr to run the function for all 5
final_results <- map_dfr(target_artists, get_artist_data)

print(final_results)


```
